summary: Verified edit_lines tool implementation end-to-end. All 18 unit tests pass, all 4 integration tests pass, and read_files line numbering test passes. Health check returns healthy. Frontend login works with test credentials. edit_lines is properly wired in the agent loop.

minor.issue: In agent-tools.js lines 69-79, when sandbox file read fails and falls back to projectFiles array, addLineNumbers() is NOT called. This means files read from DB fallback won't have line numbers for edit_lines.

integration_issues.issue: Console shows '[authFetch] getSession error: Cannot destructure property data' but login still works

action_items: OPTIONAL: Add addLineNumbers() call to fallback path in agent-tools.js lines 73-79 for consistency
critical_code_review_comments: edit_lines tool is properly defined in tools.js (lines 42-90) with correct schema
edit_lines is included in effectiveToolSet for self-edit mode (message-stream.js line 1471)
read_files returns line numbers for both self-edit (line 108) and sandbox (line 63) modes
Agent loop nudge after read_files correctly mentions edit_lines (line 2822)
edit_lines routing in message-stream.js (lines 2853-2891) properly saves to DB and handles errors
context_for_next_testing_agent: edit_lines tool is fully implemented and working. Key files: agent-tools.js (handleEditLines at line 224), message-stream.js (routing at line 2853), tools.js (schema at line 42). All unit and integration tests pass. Minor issue: fallback path in handleReadFiles doesn't add line numbers but this is edge case.

login_flow.note: Login with REDACTED_LEAKED_USER / REDACTED_LEAKED_PASSWORD works, redirects to dashboard

edit_lines_in_toolset.verified: edit_lines is in effectiveToolSet filter array
